message:
feat(ffs-repair): repair_writes_enabled gate on ScrubWithRecovery (bd-4h62v)

bd-4h62v calls for wiring ScrubDaemon into ffs mount lifecycle with
read-only-safe defaults — that requires the pipeline to support
detection-only mode where scrub evidence and corruption records
still flow but no repair-side writes touch the device. This commit
lands the pipeline-side gate; the mount-lifecycle wiring lands
separately in ffs-cli once the gate is available.

Add repair_writes_enabled: bool field on ScrubWithRecovery, defaults
to true in ScrubWithRecovery::new so existing callers see no
behavior change. Add a with_repair_writes_enabled(enabled: bool)
const builder so read-only mounts can configure detection-only mode
fluently:

  ScrubWithRecovery::new(...)
      .with_atomic_metrics(metrics)
      .with_repair_writes_enabled(false)   // read-only mount

Gate every site that mutates the device behind the new flag without
disturbing the detection/observation paths:

- on_group_write(): early-return Ok(()) before maybe_refresh_dirty_group
  when writes are disabled. The mark_group_dirty_with_cause("write")
  step still runs so dirty tracking remains accurate for the
  read-only observer.
- on_group_flush(): early-return Ok(()) before maybe_refresh_dirty_group
  when writes are disabled. Read-only mounts should never trigger a
  symbol-refresh write from a fsync/flush hook.
- Scrub completion path: skip refresh_dirty_groups_now when writes
  are disabled. update_adaptive_policy still runs (policy tuning is
  in-memory) and sync_atomic_staleness_gauge still runs (read-only
  metric).
- Recovery loop: when writes are disabled, skip recover_group and
  instead mark every block in corrupt_blocks as
  BlockOutcome::Unrecoverable, increment total_unrecoverable, and
  call self.observe_corrupt_group(...) so the corruption record
  gets emitted via the existing observation channel — the user-
  visible read-only contract is "we will tell you what we found,
  but we will not write".

The gate is additive (default true) so all existing callers
continue to recover/refresh exactly as before. Tests for
ScrubDaemon's mount-lifecycle wiring, the read-only flag flow at
the CLI surface, and the asupersync cancellation/region-ownership
glue are tracked under bd-4h62v and land alongside the ffs-cli
mount_cmd integration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
